title: HAIR Embassy HAL Generation Profile
description: An optional generation-oriented profile that binds HAIR structure, semantics, physical facts, and MCU topology into an auditable contract for Embassy-style HAL code generation. The same approved profile may lower either to an embedded-target Embassy HAL crate or to a separate host-emulated companion crate. Generated driver APIs must be derived from explicit referenced lowering inputs rather than from fixed placeholder method sets.

loweringPattern.description: Optional generator-facing lowering selector for cases where one supported driver kind has more than one approved evidence-backed lowering family with materially different bring-up behavior. The first reserved value, `serial-jtag-preserve-link`, selects a USB Serial/JTAG-style lowering that preserves an already boot-established link instead of synthesizing a reset-and-reattach sequence. Generators must fail explicitly if a selected lowering pattern lacks the referenced semantic inputs it requires.

packageName.description: Base embedded package name for generated Embassy output. Host-emulated generation derives its package name automatically from this base in the first cut.

crateName.description: Base embedded crate name for generated Embassy output. Host-emulated generation derives its crate name automatically from this base in the first cut.

allOf.description: A generator-facing driver instance. Executable behavior must be justified by the referenced topology and semantic records plus structural register/field data reachable either directly or through explicit resolvable structural relationships. When lowered through host-emulated generation, every generated HAL-visible driver surface derived from this instance must also produce a paired emulator/state handle.

targetRef.description: For the first Embassy generator cut, this should resolve to a canonical block in profiles.mcuSoc that the driver instance lowers from.

modulePath.description: Generator-facing Rust module placement for the emitted driver/support code. This does not by itself define a fixed method set; methods must be derived from the referenced lowering inputs. Support-oriented placements such as a generated time module are allowed only when the referenced driver instance carries the explicit lowering contract needed for that support surface. The same placement governs host-emulated lowering for the paired HAL and emulator surfaces.

clockBindingRefs.description: Referenced clock bindings that may justify emitted clock-gating or bring-up helpers when those bindings and their linked structural/semantic data are sufficient for real lowering.

resetBindingRefs.description: Referenced reset bindings that may justify emitted reset assert/release helpers when those bindings and their linked structural/semantic data are sufficient for real lowering.

interruptRouteRefs.description: Referenced interrupt routes required by any emitted interrupt-driven API surface. Naming these routes does not permit the generator to invent unsupported async behavior.

dmaRouteRefs.description: Referenced DMA routes required by any emitted DMA-backed API surface. The generator must fail explicitly if a claimed DMA-backed behavior lacks the additional lowering data needed for real code generation.

pinRoles.description: Pin-role declarations used to bind generator-facing driver roles to explicit pin-topology routes. These support emitted pin/routing code only when the referenced routes and linked structure carry enough detail for real lowering.

initOperationRefs.description: Semantic operations that define emitted bring-up, calibration, enable, or other executable helpers. These are lowering inputs, not documentation-only labels, and should target the same hardware block as the driver instance.

stateMachineRefs.description: Semantic state machines that constrain emitted mode-transition helpers. These are lowering inputs, not documentation-only labels, and first-cut lowering expects the transition effect being emitted to target a field on the same hardware block as the driver instance.

capabilityTags.description: Generator-facing capability annotations. Capability tags do not authorize the generator to emit behavior that is unsupported by the referenced topology and semantic lowering inputs. The first-cut profile reserves `embassy-time-driver` to mark the unique interrupt driver instance that provides generated SysTick-backed Embassy async timing support when the document explicitly carries the required interrupt-path and startup lowering data.
